programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: After being bitten by a genetically-modified spider, a shy teenager gains spider-like abilities that he uses to fight injustice as a masked superhero and face a vengeful enemy.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: After a faulty interview with the Life Foundation ruins his career, former reporter Eddie Brock's life is in pieces. Six months later, he comes across the Life Foundation again, and he comes into contact with an alien symbiote and becomes Venom, a parasitic antihero.

programme.title: John Wick
programme.desc: John Wick is a former hitman grieving the loss of his true love. When his home is broken into, robbed, and his dog killed, he is forced to return to action to exact revenge.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 2
programme.desc: John Wick is forced out of retirement by a former associate looking to seize control of a shadowy international assassins’ guild. Bound by a blood oath to aid him, Wick travels to Rome and does battle against some of the world’s most dangerous killers.

programme.title: Tombstone
programme.desc: After success cleaning up Dodge City, Wyatt Earp moves to Tombstone, Arizona, and wishes to get rich in obscurity. He meets his brothers there, as well as his old friend Doc Holliday. A band of outlaws that call themselves The Cowboys are causing problems in the region with various acts of random violence, and inevitably come into confrontation with Holliday and the Earps, which leads to a shoot-out at the O.K. Corral.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Raiders of the Lost Ark
programme.desc: When Dr. Indiana Jones – the tweed-suited professor who just happens to be a celebrated archaeologist – is hired by the government to locate the legendary Ark of the Covenant, he finds himself up against the entire Nazi regime.

programme.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
programme.desc: An apocalyptic story set in the furthest reaches of our planet, in a stark desert landscape where humanity is broken, and most everyone is crazed fighting for the necessities of life. Within this world exist two rebels on the run who just might be able to restore order.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Mechanic: Resurrection
programme.desc: Arthur Bishop thought he had put his murderous past behind him when his most formidable foe kidnaps the love of his life. Now he is forced to travel the globe to complete three impossible assassinations, and do what he does best, make them look like accidents.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: An art collector appeals to Indiana Jones to embark on a search for the Holy Grail. He learns that another archaeologist has disappeared while searching for the precious goblet, and the missing man is his own father, Dr. Henry Jones. The artifact is much harder to find than they expected, and its powers are too much for those impure of heart.

programme.title: Baby Driver
programme.desc: Coerced into working for a crime boss, a young getaway driver must face the music when a doomed heist threatens his life, love, and freedom.

programme.title: Constantine
programme.desc: John Constantine has literally been to Hell and back. When he teams up with a policewoman to solve the mysterious suicide of her twin sister, their investigation takes them through the world of demons and angels that exists beneath the landscape of contemporary Los Angeles.

programme.title: The Punisher
programme.desc: After Frank Castle's whole family is gunned down in cold blood, he shall become the vengeful Angel of Death known as the Great Equaliser of Crime: The Punisher. The one-man hurricane of blood and bullets is about to make the thugs who destroyed his life rue the day they made The Punisher come to life.

programme.title: Private Parts
programme.desc: Having always wanted to be a disc-jockey, Howard Stern works his way painfully from radio at his 1970's college to a Detroit station. It is with a move to Washington that he hits on an outrageous off-the-wall style that catches audience attention. Despite his on-air blue talk, at home he is a loving husband. He needs all the support he can get when he joins NBC in New York and comes up against a very different vision of radio.

programme.title: Police Academy 4: Citizens on Patrol
programme.desc: A new batch of recruits arrives at Police Academy, this time a group of civilian volunteers who have joined Commandant Lassard's new Citizens on Patrol program. Although the community relations project has strong governmental support, a disgusted Captain Harris is determined to see it fail.

programme.title: Trading Places
programme.desc: A snobbish Wall Street commodities broker and a wily street hustler find their positions reversed as part of a bet by two callous millionaire brothers who run a brokerage house.

programme.title: My Cousin Vinny
programme.desc: Two carefree pals traveling through rural Alabama on their way back to college are mistakenly arrested and charged with murder. Fortunately, one of them has a cousin who's a lawyer - Vincent Gambini, a former auto mechanic from Brooklyn who has just passed his bar exam after his sixth try. When he arrives with his leather-clad girlfriend to try his first case, it's a real shock - for him and the Deep South!

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: A married workaholic, Michael Newman doesn't have time for his wife and children, not if he's to impress his ungrateful boss and earn a well-deserved promotion. So when he meets Morty, a loopy sales clerk, he gets the answer to his prayers: a magical remote that allows him to bypass life's little distractions with increasingly hysterical results.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head Do the Universe
programme.desc: After a "creative" judge sentences them to space camp, a black hole sends our adolescent heroes 24 years into the modern future where the duo misuse iPhones, embark on a quest to score, and become targets of the Deep State.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Police Academy: Mission to Moscow
programme.desc: The Russians seek help in dealing with the Mafia from the veterans of the Police Academy. They head off to Moscow, in order to find evidence against Konstantin Konali, who marketed a computer game that everyone in the world is playing.

programme.title: Police Academy
programme.desc: The mayor declares that anyone can enroll in the police academy irrespective of their physical condition or education level, and many misfits descend on the academy, including a young troublemaker who's forced to apply or go to jail.

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
programme.desc: Six Police Academy graduates are assigned to the 16th precinct, and their Captain desperately wants them to clean up the crime ridden neighborhood, but his second in command schemes to become captain himself by sabotaging the rookies.

programme.title: The Princess Bride
programme.desc: In this enchantingly cracked fairy tale, the beautiful Princess Buttercup and the dashing Westley must overcome staggering odds to find happiness amid six-fingered swordsmen, murderous princes, Sicilians and rodents of unusual size. But even death can't stop these true lovebirds from triumphing.

programme.title: Hot Shots!
programme.desc: Topper Harley, a top gun fighter pilot, is recalled to serve on the SS Essess. Topper's mission is to destroy Saddam Hussein's nuclear plants. Unfortunately, Topper is psychologically imbalanced and is sure to crack under pressure.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Ace Ventura: Pet Detective
programme.desc: To be a Pet Detective, you have to understand both the criminals and animals. Ace Ventura goes even further... He behaves like a criminal animal. When a football team's mascot (a dolphin) is stolen just before the Superbowl, Ace Ventura is put on the case. Now, who would want to steal a dolphin, and why?

programme.title: Grown Ups
programme.desc: After their high school basketball coach passes away, five good friends and former teammates reunite for a Fourth of July holiday weekend.

programme.title: Borat
programme.desc: Kazakh journalist Borat Sagdiyev travels to America to make a documentary. As he zigzags across the nation, Borat meets real people in real situations with hysterical consequences. His backwards behavior generates strong reactions around him exposing prejudices and hypocrisies in American culture.

programme.title: Lady Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Frankenstein's daughter and his assistant/her lover continue his experiments in an attempt to rebuild his legacy after he is killed by his psychotic, murderous first monster.

programme.title: Night of the Living Dead
programme.desc: A ragtag group of Pennsylvanians barricade themselves in an old farmhouse to remain safe from a horde of flesh-eating ghouls that are ravaging the Northeast of the United States.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, Dracula's tortured henchman, is forced to capture prey for his master and do his every bidding. But now, after centuries of servitude, Renfield is ready to see if there is a life outside his boss's shadow.

programme.title: Son of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Returning to the ancestral castle long after the death of the monster, the son of Dr. Frankenstein meets a mad shepherd who is hiding the comatose creature. To clear the family name, he revives the creature and tries to rehabilitate him.

programme.title: Soylent Green
programme.desc: A nightmarish futuristic fantasy about the controlling power of big corporations and an innocent cop who stumbles on the truth.

programme.title: Terror Train
programme.desc: A college fraternity prank goes wrong and a student ends up in the mental asylum. Three years later, it's graduation time, and the members of the fraternity decide to have a costume party aboard a train trip to celebrate their graduation. Unknowingly to them, a killer has slipped aboard, killing them off one by one, disguised in the costumes of the victims.

programme.title: The Dead Don't Die
programme.desc: The focus is on the sleepy small town of Centerville where the dead become undead.

programme.title: The Ghost of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Ygor resurrects Frankenstein's monster and brings him to the original doctor's son Ludwig for help. Ludwig, obsessed with the idea of restoring the monster to full power, is unaware that his various associates all have different ideas about whose brain should be transplanted into the monster's skull.

programme.title: The Invisible Man
programme.desc: A scientist finds a way of becoming invisible, but in doing so, he becomes murderously insane.

programme.title: The Invisible Man Returns
programme.desc: The owner of a coal mining operation, falsely imprisoned for fratricide, takes a drug to make him invisible, despite its side effect: gradual madness.

programme.title: The Invisible Man's Revenge
programme.desc: An eager scientist tests his new formula for invisibility on an escaped fugitive. When the formula works the criminal runs off to terrorize a family he believes cheated him out of a fortune years earlier.

programme.title: The Invisible Ray
programme.desc: Dr. Janos Rukh discovers a certain type of radium that has almost magical healing properties. But the element has a dangerous side, too, and it has already started affecting Rukh. Consumed by paranoia, he begins to suspect that his wife is having an affair. Wild for revenge, Rukh hatches a deadly plot...using his own poisoned body as a weapon to kill.

programme.title: The Invisible Woman
programme.desc: Eccentric Professor Gibbs, brilliant but impractical, invents an invisibility machine and advertises for a guinea pig. What he gets is Kitty Carroll, an attractive, adventurous model, who thinks being invisible would help her settle a few scores. Complications arise when three comic gangsters steal the machine to use on their boss. But they fail to reckon with the Revenge of the Invisible Woman!

programme.title: The Little Shop of Horrors
programme.desc: A clumsy young man working at an impoverished flower shop discovers that the strange plant he has been nurturing has an insatiable appetite for blood, forcing him to kill to feed it.

programme.title: The Monkey
programme.desc: When twin brothers Bill and Hal find their father's old monkey toy in the attic, a series of gruesome deaths start. The siblings decide to throw the toy away and move on with their lives, growing apart over the years.

programme.title: The Monolith Monsters
programme.desc: Rocks from a meteor which grow when in contact with water threaten a sleepy Southwestern desert community.

programme.title: The Mummy
programme.desc: An ancient Egyptian priest named Imhotep is revived when a British archaeological expedition finds his mummy and one of the researchers accidentally reads an ancient life-giving spell. Imhotep escapes from the field site and searches for the reincarnation of the soul of his lover.

programme.title: The Mummy's Curse
programme.desc: An irrigation project in the rural bayous of Louisiana unearths living mummy Kharis, who was buried in quicksand twenty-five years earlier.

programme.title: The Mummy's Ghost
programme.desc: An Egyptian high priest travels to America to reclaim the bodies of ancient Egyptian princess Ananka and her living guardian mummy Kharis. Learning that Ananka's spirit has been reincarnated into another body, he kidnaps a young woman of Egyptian descent with a mysterious resemblance to the princess. However, the high priest's greedy desires cause him to lose control of the mummy.

programme.title: Oingo Boingo Live At The Ritz (1985)
John Lennon and the plastic ono band Toronto 1969
Van Halen - Live Without a Net (1986)
Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers - Rockpalast (1977)
Jethro Tull - Jack In The Green
ACDC - Live in Cleveland Full Concert (2003)
Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Kay & Edie vs. Lisa & Ginger vs. Becky & Tom
programme.desc: Kay & Edie would later appear in the Sweep Of Champions.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Jill & Tina vs. Dina Rae & Desirae vs. Carol & Lance
programme.desc: Carol & Lance would later appear in the Sweep Of Champions.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in the middle ages, the stooges wish to marry their sweethearts, but the King won't give his consent until Princess Alicia gets married. The princess is abducted by Mergatroyd, an evil magician who plans to marry her and become ruler of the country. The stooges help the princess escape and then defeat the magician and his henchmen in a sword fight.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc:
The Stooges awake in the middle of the night to a crying baby left on their doorstep. A letter from the despondent mother (Julie Gibson) states that baby Jimmie (Joyce Gardner) has been abandoned. The Stooges react by taking the little guy in, feeding him, and trying their best to act fatherly.
When the mother and two motorcycle cops come to reclaim the baby the Stooges evacuate their home quickly, with Jimmie in tow; unbeknownst to the stooges Jimmie crawled into the back seat of their car. The police on their motorcycles track them down and the baby is returned to the parents who, the father being one of the cops, reconcile. The Stooges, meanwhile, make a conspicuous escape by skittering away hidden in large haystacks.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are salesman selling a weight reducing machine. They have no luck until they show up at the house of an eccentric inventor where they are hired as caretakers. When the scientist goes to Washington to demonstrate his death-ray machine to the government, the boys are left to guard his house and must contend with enemy spies and a mysterious pie thrower.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Set in a desert land where the stooges run a restaurant, the boys need money to pay their fiancée's taxes, or the girls will be sold as slaves. Some crooks come into their restaurant and convince the boys to recover the stolen Rootin Tootin diamond. The stooges decide to return the diamond to the government and get the reward money. They learn that the Emir of Shmo has absconded with the contraband jewel. They journey to the stronghold of Shmo where they disguise as Santa Clauses and scare the ruler into giving them the diamond.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges accompany professor Jones on an expedition to Venus, where they discover that the Venusians are planning to conquer the earth with an army of zombies. When the boys learn that they're going to be turned into zombies, they escape. The scene changes to the stooges apartment where we learn they are just telling a bedtime story to their kids (also played by the stooges) while they wait for the baby sitter to arrive. When the baby sitter shows up, she looks like one of the zombies and the boys exit in a hurry.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are street cleaners who find some valuable bonds and return them to their owner. The man is so grateful that he offers them a big reward if they can find an honest man with executive ability. Their search leads them to a woman who's fiancée is honest, but he's in jail. The boys decide to commit a crime so they can go behind bars to find him. In prison the boys locate the man and help him escape, only to find out that their benefactor is a con man and on the way himself to the slammer.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: When the Three Stooges agree to help Mrs.Lawrence prepare a fancy birthday party,their particular specially-an exploding,gas-filled cake - goes off with a bang.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc:
Svengoolie presents 'The Thing That Couldn't Die'.
A psychically gifted young woman discovers a centuries-old crate buried on her aunt's ranch. Opening it, her family discovers the living head of Gideon Drew, a 16th century devil worshiper who was beheaded by Sir Francis Drake.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan fear a financial crisis when they cannot sell the house they refurbished and their new partner, Roger, skips town.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan realizes that he needs a steady job to take care of his family when he accidentally knocks out one of Roseanne's teeth on the day she is to meet with a health inspector (who turns out to be Leon); Darlene is accepted to art school but David is rejected.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne worries that Darlene may be planning to run away from home; new neighbor George befriends D.J.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne is suffering from empty-nest syndrome as Darlene heads for school. But after Darlene gets settled into her dorm, the two have a revealing mother-daughter chat, and Roseanne returns home with a plan.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Brutally honest Roseanne suffers the consequences after telling Bev that she's had her fill of her at the Lunch Box. Meanwhile, Jackie jumps back into the dating arena.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Jackie and Roseanne plan a housewarming party for Jackie's new home and try to hide it from their mom. Roseanne invites Darlene home for the weekend to get her back together with David. D.J. goes through a rebellious period of skipping school and enlisting Darlene's help in covering for him, and Roseanne is determined to find out what's going on. Fred pursues Jackie after their one-night stand.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Foreman Dan feels lower than low for getting tough with his employees at work, while Roseanne lectures David for getting high after she finds a stash of pot in the house -- then realizes it is hers from decades ago.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: After months of trying to get pregnant, Roseanne learns that Jackie is expecting -- and Fred is the unknowing father.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: A reorganization within the store forces the staff of the Gentlemens Ready-To-Wear Department to share their floor space with the Ladies Department. Mrs. Slocombe, head of the Ladies, and Mr. Grainger, head of the Gents, immediately come to loggerheads over control of the center floor display. Mr. Grainger refuses to take down his trousers and put Mrs. Slocombe's underwear in its place. Undaunted, she goes over his head to Captain Peacock, and then to Mr. Rumbold.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr. Lucas is keen on Miss Brahms and decides to write her a note to ask her out. But when Mrs. Slocombe gets the note instead, the situation gets very complicated. To top it off, she thinks Captain Peacock sent her the note. Oh what a day it's going to be.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill's Dad tries to decorate the Christmas tree, but he gets locked in the garage.
Featured cartoons:
Goldimouse and the Three Cats (1960) One Ham's Family (1943) Sleepy-Time Squirrel (1954) Christmas Comes But Once A Year (1936) Rabbit's Feat (1960)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: A surgeon has an ingenious plan for murdering his partner in a research project, but a paranoid nurse quickly catches onto his scheme, so he kills her. Lt. Columbo has a tough time figuring this one out.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude finds a wallet on the street that belongs to a man, known for his infidelities. After Walter declares a wallet is a place of privacy for all men, she snoops into his wallet. This triggers a series of mistaken identities when she confronts a woman, Norma, whom she thinks is carrying on an affair with Walter.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Walter throws Maude a surprise birthday party and she truly is surprised but unhappy about turning another year older. Tired of the men going off by themselves and the women going off by themselves, Maude makes Walter promise to not leave her side the entire evening. However, he soon breaks his promise and Maude goes into her bedroom and refuses to come out.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Sophia's latest boyfriend, Rocco claims to have been involved in the mob, so when the girls discover a bag of Rocco's with thousands of dollars in it, they assume he robbed a bank. Meanwhile, Dorothy and Blanche sneak a peak at Rose's diary.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Rose's latest boyfriend has just retired and she is ready to spend a whole lot more time with him but his retirement quickly turns him into a couch potato. Meanwhile, the girls deal with remodeling the garage into a guest room.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy's son, Michael pays another visit to Miami and this time he has an announcement, he's engaged. However, Dorothy has two more surprises in store, her future daughter-in-law is also black and twenty years older than Michael.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Rose is enamored with a hotel guest who looks exactly like Charlie; Blanche is horrified to discover her visiting son Matthew wants to pursue a career as a comedian.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: A 9 year old runaway child shows up on the Sanfords' property and Fred is against having any little kids around to cause problems but Fred quickly lightens up the young boy despite his gruff exterior. Fred then takes it upon himself to teach the kid to appreciate what he's got at home.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike and Archie's have a long talk after they find themself locked in the storeroom of Archie's Place.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Mike and Gloria are sure the romance has gone out of their marriage when even a weekend in the Poconous fails to reignite the spark.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie's estranged brother returns, after twenty-nine years, to smooth things over before he enters the hospital for a serious operation.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Linda & Vicki vs. Deanna & Michelle vs. Sue & Dan

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George tries to come up with a good comeback after someone insults him. Elaine falls in love with a mysterious employee at the video store. Kramer wants to die a certain way. Jerry takes advice from a tennis player who stinks.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Gang members protest George's handling of a scholarship named for Susan; some find Jerry's new girlfriend objectionable; Peterman wants Kramer's life experiences for his own autobiography, written by Elaine.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: In an attempt to bond with their daughters, Dan goes clothes shopping with Becky, and Roseanne watches basketball with Darlene.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne finally has a place to write when Dan builds her an office for her birthday -- but she's stricken with a case of writer's block.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Roy stopped by Pam's desk to let her know how excited he was to see her art show. She reminded him that it was just a small show being put on by her art class. Pam later admitted she was very happy she was back together with Roy because it showed maturity. Jim feigns indifference to the reconciliation.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael was excited to be attending a party at his CFO's house. He carpooled with Dwight and was also looking forward to meeting Jan at the party. Jan wanted to spend a night alone with Michael, but he thought it was important to come out as a couple to all of their co-workers.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes "Pie Man", a masked vigilante who delivers a pie in the face of justice to evildoers. However, after attacking Mr. Burns, Burns hires him as a personal hitman, until Homer is ordered to attack the Dalai Lama.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge recall the story of their first kiss at a summer camp when they were children—an event which was not all happy, as Homer (unwillingly) betrayed Marge by standing her up on their second date.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After receiving an injection from Dr. Hibbert, Bart becomes temporarily deaf, which makes him impervious to being bullied, until he accidentally moons the American flag during a donkey basketball game, prompting everyone in town to accuse the Simpsons of being unpatriotic under the new "Government Knows Best" Act.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa makes her own newspaper for publishing poems and such, and at the same time Mr Burns decides to buy every media in town to improve his image. In the end Lisa's newspaper is the only media in town not owned by Burns, which inspires the town's inhabitants to create their own newspapers.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Ned Flanders' head injury gives him the power to predict others' deaths, Bart and Lisa play detective when a string of Victorian-era prostitutes are murdered by Jack the Ripper, and the Simpsons go on a fantastic voyage inside Mr. Burns' body to save Maggie.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Jefferson has joined the National Guard. He tells Al and Griff of the benefits: for one weekend per month, one gets to party with a bunch of guys and even get paid for it. Of course, you have to pass basic training first, but even that has a benefit. One does not have to go to one's regular job, but the boss still has to pay the salary. Al and Griff suddenly feel the urge to serve their country.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: Breaking Up Is Easy to Do (1)
programme.desc: Al and Peggy split up after seeing a marriage counselor. Kelly must learn to box to get a part in a movie.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: Breaking Up Is Easy to Do (2)
programme.desc: Al makes true of his threat to move out. But what about custody of the two TVs? The kids Al leaves to Peg. In foresight, he has hidden a nest egg in a secret compartment in the toilet seat. Peg becomes depressed, but the kids decide to find themselves a new step-dad. Marcy is ecstatic about Al's departure and has another plan to cheer Peg up. Meanwhile, Al is trying out the bachelor lifestyle, complete with a new look.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.sub-title: Breaking Up Is Easy to Do (3)
programme.desc: Al is having difficulty adjusting to being single. Marcy is more than happy to share with him that Peg is having a dinner party with a millionaire. Al doesn't believe it, so Marcy tells him that what Peg really wants is for Al to come to the dinner party.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy, jealous that Al is spending all his time at The Jiggly Room, decides to try out on amateur night while Al and the No Ma'am boys judge it. She wins the competition dressed as a harem girl named Jasmin, wearing a veil over her face. Al is turned on like never before, and he and Peggy have their best sex ever.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When Kelly becomes successful on a kid's television show, she becomes a total bitch and control freak. Her manager, Bud, decides Princess Kelly should be taught a lesson she will not forget. Meanwhile, Al tries to avoid sharing a bed with Peg by sawing off half of their bed as she is tempted to have sex with him in bed.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Marcy gets Jefferson a membership to the health club and he becomes a spectacle there. Meanwhile, Bud directs when Al and Kelly star in a commercial for the shoe store.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Marcy wants the Bundys to stay indoors because her "identical" cousin Mandy is coming for a visit and Marcy does not want her to be repulsed by them. Mandy's flight, however, is early and she takes a cab. Since the D'Arcys are at the airport waiting for her, she comes to the Bundys. This leads to Mandy spending the day out with Al and Jefferson. Suddenly Peg and Marcy find themselves jealous.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont pays twenty bucks for a commode that he thinks is a valuable antique.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: A 9 year old runaway child shows up on the Sanfords' property and Fred is against having any little kids around to cause problems but Fred quickly lightens up the young boy despite his gruff exterior. Fred then takes it upon himself to teach the kid to appreciate what he's got at home.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont and Rollo answer an ad that promises them stardom through low-budget movies. Fred gets a hold of the brochure and decides to try out too. When they get down there they quickly discover the low budget movies are X-rated followed by a police raid and Fred, Lamont and Rollo are hauled off to jail.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred comes home to find Lamont and his drama teacher Marilyn acting out a choking scene from the play "Othello." Fred then jumps to the logical conclusion that Lamont is trying to kill a white woman. Later, Fred becomes the victim of assumption when Marilyn invites Fred and Lamont to her place and her parents think Fred and Lamont are trying to rob the place.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: When his pen-pal gets released from prison, she comes to stay with Chris. She takes him hostage when the police come after her when she starts criminal activities under Chris' nose.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris decides on a whim to become a male escort, hoping for free plays and dates with young women. What he gets is a elderly woman who is hard up.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Meek, little inventor Calvin Q. Calculus defies the ranting of his battle-ax wife and invents a portable hole, which will revolutionize dog bone storage, rescue of babies from safes, and golfer putts. But a thief steals Calculus' supply of portable holes and uses them to go on a crime spree.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Among the strategies that fail in Wile E. Coyote's attempts to catch the Roadrunner: glue on the road, a giant rubber band, an outboard motor in a wash tub, and dressing in drag as a female Roadrunner.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: As the Gophers prepare for winter their tree is cut down and taken to a saw mill. When their home is made into furniture, they follow the delivery truck and take back everything that was made from their tree.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Ralph Wolf wants to steal sheep; Sam Sheepdog wants to stop him. Ralph's tricks include digging a tunnel, walking a tightrope, launching a guided missile, dressing as Little Bo Peep, shooting a cannon and growing Sam's hair.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Porky and Sylvester's desert campground is sliced away and towed into outer space by a green, bird-like Jupiterian searching for earthly animal life. But Porky remains blissfully unaware, leaving Sylvester to be terrorized by the alien.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs Bunny gets himself adopted as a pet by a kindly soul who has been feeding him carrots every day. Bugs' friend turns out to be Dr. Jekyll who drinks his evil potion and turns into a monster.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Starving Mexican mice want access to a cheese factory guarded by Sylvester Cat and send for Speedy Gonzales, the fastest mouse in all Mexico, to breeze past Sylvester and obtain the cheese for them.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: When an apple falls on Bugs' head, he's knocked into medieval times and immediately meets (and aggravates) The Black Knight. He also meets Merlin the Magician who transforms Bugs into different animals. At the end, Bugs isn't sure it was a dream.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A workman finds a singing frog in the cornerstone of an old building being demolished. But when he tries to cash in on his discovery, he finds the frog will sing only for him, and just croak for the talent agent and the audience in the theater he's spent his life savings on.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis sees the face of Jesus in a nacho chip, setting him on an epic journey to seek answers to the big question - "Why do I never score?"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to the ""Creatures of Rock"" concert and spend the whole time waiting in line for the toilet.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Imitating a PBS documentary about Ben Franklin, Beavis and Butt-head get struck by lightning, and attract the interest of ""Betsy Wiener"" from ""Decency In Media.""

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go to door-to-door to collect money for the poor as part of a class project; they meet up with a dominatrix.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys ditch school and work as temps at a real estate agency (and break stuff).

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get stuck on top of Tom Anderson's house.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: The Future of Beavis and Butt-head
programme.desc: The school's guidance counselor tries to help the boys figure out what their futures hold.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-head orders a mail-order bride from Russia to ""get some;"" Beavis wants to watch.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis & Butt-head have to change the oil in the fryer; luckily, there's a gas station next door with plenty of oil... videos; Live - "I Alone" Ween - "I Can't Put My Finger On It" Rancid - "Nihilism"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Two babes want the boys to change their flat tire, Butt-head learns what not to do with a tire-iron.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee tries to entertain himself and his playhouse friends during a rainy day, by playing games, making hot chocolate, telling a ghost story, watching cartoons and even making prank phone calls, but eventually he suffers from "playhouse fever" before wishing away the rainy day.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.sub-title: Now You See Me, Now You Don't
programme.desc: Today's secret word is really "little," but the gangs' eyes get really big when Pee-wee puts on a magic show and disappears! Captain Carl, Cowboy Curtis, Miss Yvonne, and the gang are amazed as Pee-wee makes use of his new talent at their expense, but when their hidden host can't make himself reappear, it'll take Jambi's magic to set things right!

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max, Caroline, Earl and Han help Oleg move into Sophie’s apartment until she begins turning away all of his things.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are arrested for breaking into a cute guy’s apartment to recover the rings Caroline left behind after she spent the night.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline get into a legal battle with two prep school teens who knocked off their cupcake T-shirts and are selling them.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: Brutus “The Barber” Beefcake battles “Macho Man” Randy Savage in the featured match, courtesy of Saturday Night’s Main Event. Bobby “The Brain” Heenan welcomes another eclectic group of guests to “The Bobby Heenan Show."

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Bob notices that he has started to lose his hair. In a wave of nostalgia, Linda reminisces about Bob’s mustache and how it made her fall for him years ago. Meanwhile, the kids tell their own versions of the story of how their parents met.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray realizes that his kids would rather spend time with their Uncle Robert than with him, he decides to do something about the situation. When Robert makes the observation that Ray finds it boring to be around his kids, Debra couldn't agree more and suggests that Ray follow Robert's example.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gets upset when she finds out that her parents have separated.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: While Arthur is hospitalized, Carrie begins to doubt her love for him when she discovers that, for selfish reasons, he concealed a college acceptance letter to her that would have gotten her into her dream university after high school.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: After Arthur's (played by Jerry Stiller) surgery, Carrie (Leah Remini) decides it would be a good idea to get life insurance and to make sure Arthur has somewhere to go if one of them dies. When she finds out Doug (Kevin James) wouldn't want him if she dies she gets upset. She starts smoking again and Doug thinks she is trying to die first.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer becomes the spokesman for a woman George wants to avoid; Jerry's bookie welches; a co-worker mistakes Elaine for someone else, who's reported as a problem worker.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: An ailing man suffers from blackouts and sleepwalks, leading the team to wonder if his sleepwalking is a symptom, or how the man is becoming exposed to something else. When the man's daughter grows ill as well, the team must provide a diagnosis before both die. Meanwhile, Cuddy adopts a newborn but when the birth mother displays a rash, she has to make a decision between putting the mother or daughter at risk.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In this clip show episode, Homer is honored at a Friars' Club Roast. A number of characters show up to roast him, but the celebrating is interrupted by Kang and Kodos, who say that humanity will be judged based on Homer's experiences.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons go to a Monster truck rally that features famous daredevil Lance Murdock. Bart immediately becomes enamored and decides that he wants to become a daredevil as well. His first stunt ends in injury, but to gain the respect of his friends, he promises a far more impressive feat to them: to jump the Springfield Gorge.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After hearing that Skinner is getting cold feet about their wedding, Edna leaves Skinner at the altar and, after sending back one marriage gift, hooks up with Comic Book Guy. Meanwhile, Homer and Marge have an argument about if their marriage is still solid or not.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A midlife crisis prompts Homer to quit his job and become an inventor.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: LIsa is invited to join Springfield's MENSA group but,after they are chased out of their meeting place, they go to complain to Quimby. Fearing they have exposed his corruption he flees town. An ancient charter then reveals that in such cases the town should be ruled by the smartest people so the MENSA group take office. However,their elitist rules are very unpopular and Stephen Hawking has to intervene to save then from the mob.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart learns that he was once a child actor in commercials and that all the money earned was spent by Homer. In retaliation, Bart petitions the court to have himself legally emancipated from his parents and moves out of the Simpson house. Bart is initially miserable in his new apartment, but discovers that skateboarder Tony Hawk lives on one of the floors above. He and Hawk become friends, and Bart is content with his new life. Homer apologizes to Bart and requests that he return home. Bart accepts the apology, but tells his family that he is going on tour with Hawk. At an event, Homer speaks to Bart and finally promises Bart that he will never treat him badly again.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Wishing to retire and pursue other interests, Mr. Burns sells the nuclear power plant to two German businessmen. The new owners evaluate the plant and its employees with the goal of making it more efficient and decide to lay off Homer. Meanwhile, Mr. Burns finds retirement to be unsatisfying.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's juvenile antics at the speedway track result in Maude Flanders falling to her death. For once Homer is genuinely sorry for his bereaved neighbour and attempts to help Ned find a new lady-friend via video dating. However, it does not work and Ned is in danger of losing his faith - until he meets the lead singer in a Christian rock band, Rachel Jordan, with whom he strikes up a new friendship.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a ruined movie night, thanks to Bart and Lisa, Homer and Marge take a vacation away from them, but decide to go to Miami instead of going to see an uncle. When Bart and Lisa catch wind of their parents' secret plan, Bart steals Rod's credit card (just like Homer stole Ned's) and go after them.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish
programme.desc: Bart catches a three-eyed fish in the river near Springfield Nuclear Power Plant. When the fish story is featured prominently in the local news, the plant is inspected and then fined millions of dollars for its many safety violations. Infuriated, Mr. Burns decides to run for governor so he can do away with the regulations that are costing him so much money.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: Mankind cements his legacy in WWE history as he battles The Rock for the WWE Championship. Commissioner Shawn Michaels turns the tables on Mr. McMahon and The Corporation with the help of some old friends, but soon learns what goes around comes around. Plus, Ken Shamrock faces Steve Blackman and Chyna has a special friend for Mark Henry.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira attend Roland’s annual Hawaiian bash. Alexis and Ted throw a couple’s dinner party, while David and Stevie’s relationship heats up.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira's estranged sister, Deedee, pays an unexpected visit, while David agrees to talk with one of Jocelyn's students about being "different".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: A handsome businessman returns Max and Caroline’s missing cat, Nancy, who comes home with a big surprise.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline attempt to bring in extra money to help pay off their bank loan by taking second jobs as a pastry chef and a hostess at a chic restaurant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max pursues Nashit, a handsome new waiter at The High, despite Joe’s “no hook-up” rule.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Sophie takes Max and Caroline shopping for their bridesmaids’ dresses, the girls enlist the diner gang’s help to hide the ugliest ones, in the hopes of avoiding complete and utter humiliation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Look Of The Irish
programme.desc: Max and Caroline try to break Nash into the modeling business in an effort to bring in extra money acting as his managers. Also, Sophie and Oleg attempt to keep celibate for 30 days before their wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline’s attempt to break Nash into the modeling business comes to a screeching halt when his mother arrives from Ireland to retrieve her son.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline help launch the new branch of The High at JFK airport, and Sophie discovers that Oleg may have an ulterior motive for wanting to marry her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline are tasked with retrieving Sophie’s wedding dress from customs and safely transporting it to the church in time for her big wedding.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline team up with the diner staff in a frantic attempt to keep their businesses open when the city targets Han's diner and their cupcake window for destruction. Also, Sophie and Oleg announce that they are trying to have a baby.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When the shower at Max and Caroline's apartment breaks, they get part-time jobs at a fancy gym's juice bar and enjoy free use of the locker room. However, their jobs and free showers are jeopardized when Caroline has a run-in with a group of rich moms who are jealous of the attention she's getting from a sexy trainer.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline's ex Andy returns and she is denial over how they broke up. And when he informs her that he's about to get married, she offers to make the wedding cake. And she is constantly wondering if she made a mistake breaking up with him. And she tries to find wrong with him but can't.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's cupcake shop is boycotted after they refuse to sell cupcakes to "I", an artist who wants to put cocktail weenies in them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Han insists the diner gang attends a mandatory night out in an "Escape Room Club" when he feels they aren't working together as a team. However, once they're locked inside the "Alice in Wonderland"-themed room, they find Han has an ulterior motive.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Not Regular Down There
programme.desc: Max is both puzzled and curious when the new guy she is dating expresses hesitations about intimacy because he is "not regular down there." Also, Sophie and Oleg struggle to get pregnant.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline's grandmother Astrid awakens from a coma with no knowledge of her family's massive money scandal, Caroline attempts to keep the truth hidden for a bit longer by giving Astrid one last fancy luncheon.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: Colin and Isaac's friendship is tested. Roy is asked to do a press conference.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: While some of the Greyhounds head home to play for their countries in international matches, Edwin Akufo brings a business proposal to Rebecca.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Uncle Junior enjoys a romantic tryst in Boca with his girlfriend Bobbi, not realizing that she's been gabbing to her beauty-parlor friends about his oral talents. Meanwhile, controversy arises when Meadow's soccer coach announces he's leaving the school.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Christopher and Adrianna cut a deal with a famous gangsta rapper who claims that Hesh pocketed royalties belonging to a cousin who recorded several hit songs decades ago. Meanwhile, Tony plays golf with Cusamano and his white-bread friends.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Having had their memories erased by ambitious master architect Michael, Eleanor, Chidi, Tahani and Jason again settle into the Good Place, unaware of what has previously transpired...except that Eleanor discovers the one clue she had left for herself and attempts to piece things together.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael continues to work out the kinks in his grand plan. Meanwhile, Eleanor stumbles across a secret.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Six years later. With the Driftmark succession suddenly critical, Rhaenyra attempts to strike a bargain with Rhaenys.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Cersei and Jaime adjust to a world without Tywin. Varys reveals a conspiracy to Tyrion. Dany faces a new threat to her rule. Jon is caught between two kings.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: The House of Black and White
programme.desc: Arya arrives in Braavos. Podrick and Brienne run into trouble on the road. Cersei fears for her daughter's safety in Dorne as Ellaria Sand seeks revenge for Oberyn's death. Stannis tempts Jon. An adviser tempts Daenerys.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The team must treat an agoraphobic who refuses to leave his house, and have to work around Cuddy, who is less than thrilled at having hospital equipment relocated. Meanwhile, House is plagued by an itch, and Cameron and Chase have relationship issues.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren hits Stimpy in the nose and he starts to sing with a crooner-like voice. Stimpy becames famous, until his nose leaves him because of being slapped many times.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy are going to be the first dog and cat sent up to space in the Russian space program

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren is suffering from horrible allergies. Little does he know that he may be allergic to his best pal Stimpy.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy find an abandoned circus clown at their doorstep. While Ren DOES NOT want to keep him, Stimpy pleads and begs until Ren agrees.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy get jobs as bellhops and must make sure that no one finds out who is living in the top two floors of the hotel, but their curiosity gets the best of them.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go to a trailer park during a tornado and (almost) get to ""do it"" with Lolita & Tanqueray.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys win the lottery and buy a riding lawn mower.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Van Driessen has Beavis and Butt-head clean his house to learn about work and money; instead they learn that 8-track tapes aren't waterproof.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Placed in a ""Scared Straight"" program at school, the boys visit a prison and thrash with some inmates who like Judas Priest and Iron Maiden.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head enter a bratwurst eating contest and end up having to feed frogs to earn the entrance fee.

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Dangerous Duo (Joanna & Danny) vs. Challengers (Kevin & Carla)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Psychopathic Peas (David & Danielle) vs. Dirty Destroyers (Susan & Mark)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: Ross doesn't show up for work, leaving the kids to run this week's show.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Wright Brothers Lost Their Luggage will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross tries to improve the show's ratings by having this episode be the first to be shot in several different locations.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Parents and Teachers Celebrate the Cancellation of You Can't Do That On Television will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross decides to show how all of the countries around the world celebrate special events.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rambo Thinks will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Some of the cast members try to do certain things, all unsuccessfully. Nick tries to remember his name, Sariya tries to keep her new hairstyle from getting slimed, Christian tries to nice to Jennifer, and Jennifer tries to wear a dress.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Roseanne Barr Goes Synchronized Swimming will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross goes sports crazy. He makes Chris use the eternal flame to light the gas grill, causing him to blow up, he uses special effects to give Carlos athletes foot, and he uses a ""Test Your Strength"" machine to scam the cast out of money.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Green Slime is Scraped from Smokers Lungs will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast really begins to think that green slime is made from the mucus from the lungs of smokers.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Batman Gives Away His Batmobile will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
In order to get him slimed, Chris lets Patrick do the introduction. The make-up lady makes Stephanie look like a senior citizen. When the boom mike breaks, Ross makes Christian hang from the ceiling to operate the mike.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Rambo Takes Ballet Lessons will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross, in an effort to embarrass the cast, has their parents come to the studio.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Doogie Howser Gets Blamed for Sawing the Wrong Leg Off a Patient will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast is very upset that kids get blamed for everything.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
In honor of Spike TV's relationship with the the Ultimate Fighting Championship, this special edition of MXC pits the ultimate fighters against the Wisteria Lane gang and their compadres of Desperate Housewives in the battle of the octagoners and the Eva Longorias.
Games include: Take a Leek; Mudd Butlers; Sinkers and Floaters; Dash to Death; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Real Mafia vs. Video Game Industry
programme.desc:
MXC presents the goombahs and the game boys (and girls) in this battle.
Games include: Backyard Bocci Ball Bloodbath; Dope on a Rope; Nerve Racketeering; Slabs of Death; Sorry-Wrong Number; Clear Sphere of Fear

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Hollywood's Jilted Wives vs. Cheating Husbands
programme.desc:
The Brads attempt to get the upper hand on the Jennifers in an all-star edition of MXC to kickoff 2006.
Games include: Rotating Surfboard of Death; Pole Riders; Hand Job; Brass Balls

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Big Bird takes on big bets when MXC brings together two of the most polar opposites in America: The kiddie playground of Sesame Street and the adult playground known as Las Vegas.
Games include: Cruelette; Wall Buggers; Big Bird Droppings; Sinkers and Floaters

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Howard Stern's radio regulars go toe to toe against Hollywood rehab addicts and alkies...it's the classic battle of the wack pack vs. the crack pack. Games - Rotating Surfboards of Death, The Crate Escape, Sperm Whalers, and Sinkers and Floaters.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's 'Roid Rage' as the pumped up blood-doping Pro Athletes take on the Fashion industry's Super Models...it's the classic battle of Anabolic Steroids versus Anorexic mind voids! Games - Eat Shitake, Spit Ball, Irritable Bowl Syndrome, and Log Drop.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Celebrity Justice vs. TV Motor Sports Shows
programme.desc: It's Hollywood infamy and mechanical reverie as Celebrity Justice takes on TV's Motor shows. It's the classic battle of Fallen Stars and Souped-up Cars. Games - Sack Lunch, Wall Buggers, Little Man in the Boat, and Brass Balls.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: It's a cinematic bonanza as the big screen Tough Guys go head-to-head with the box office Chick Flicks...it's the classic battle of Rambo versus Romance. Games - Slippery Slope of Slanted Death, Tumbling Dominoes of Doom, Catch Hell, and Log Drop.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: While on a hill trek, Daria's class gets caught in an unexpected snowstorm.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A football injury sidelines star quarterback Kevin, forcing him to re-evaluate his life for a minute or two.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When a history test is stolen, Daria alone can solve the crime - provided she doesn't wake up.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Inspired by a lecture and the tribulations of Thomas Edison and the Wright Brothers, Mr. O'Neill encourages his class to pick something to fail at for personal growth. All his pupils get out of it instead are misery, grief, and despair, including Daria who may be forced to supervise Quinn at an upcoming mall fashion exhibit, and Jane, who's becoming so popular that she might replace Brittany as... a CHEERLEADER!!

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: While struggling to buy toilet paper for Jake, Daria gets stranded at the Lawndale High homecoming parade, where she meets Jane who plans to meet Tom so they can make fun of everybody. Instead, she runs into Tad Gupty who lost contact with his family(see "Pinch Sitter," Episode #1.8) and then Tom, who she finds is not such a bad guy after all. In fact, he may be more.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Helen has to cancel out of a business junket with Jake, guess who is coerced into taking her place?

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The Morgendorffers visit a "spa for the soul" to get their psyches massaged.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A labyrinthine discount-bulk-warehouse superstore serves as the destination for several characters. Jane goes there to buy Gummi Bears (for art supply purposes) after Tom ate hers. Daria accompanies her to buy new shoelaces, because Quinn stole the ones from her boot to repair a bag. Trent and Jesse go there to buy a headlamp and scented candles, respectively, and Tom accompanies them so he can find Jane and make up with her after their argument. (Walking with the two musicians, their half-brained purchase ideas drive him nearly up the wall.) The Fashion Club goes to the warehouse store to buy sunscreen and various other beauty needs, and, of course, their incredibly high standards prevent them from making any actual purchases. The free-sample cheese logs at the store become quite popular-an obsession, in fact, for hungry Mr. DeMartino, whose food budget is incredibly tight, and for Mrs. Johansen (a 300-pound hypoglycemic woman who is a recurring character). Kevin's dad, Doug, is holdi

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: London has been flooded. Indeed, all the sea and land on Earth have swapped places. It appears to be because the moon's gravitational pull has gone haywire, so DM and Penfold head up into outer space.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: London has been shrouded in darkness for almost a week, so Danger Mouse and Penfold head off into outer space to investigate. There they encounter a anti-logic spaceship manned by The Gremlin, who plans to turn the entire Earth Topsy turvy.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Stiletto steals a batch of recently developed passport pictures of Penfold. Il Barone proceeds to use his new puppet personality projector to take over Penfolds' mind, what little there is of it, and turn him into an assassin.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: I Spy With My Little Eye...
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to the Arctic when Greenback threatens to melt the polar ice with sun lamps.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to track down a huge hairy monster that has been crushing the local villages. When they find it they discover he's actually friendly, but suffers from extreme bouts of sore-feet. They get help from the RCMP - Royal Canadian Mounted Podiatrists.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold get a visit from Penfold's aunt, but while they were having time with her, she is suddenly snatched away, along with the Statue of Liberty from the U.S.A. D.M. and Penfold soon realize that Greenback is behind the theft and the kidnapping, and so it's up to them to stop Greenback and save Penfold's aunt, being very careful of the dangers they soon face.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Professor Crumhorn creates a robot duplicate of Penfold (which is far more efficient than the original). At the push of a button it transforms into a giant machine intent on destroying Danger Mouse.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Homemade Volcano, Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Walk Through Paper, Heat Spiral and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Homemade Volcano. Dropper Diver, How Many Stars, Heat Spiral, Heat From your Body, Paper Fold, Computer Plane and Walk Through Paper.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Bird Flies, Bleach Art, Tricks with Gravity, Fire Extinguisher and more
programme.desc: In this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Fire Writing, Paper Towel Tube, Fish Bombing Run, How a Bird Flies, Impossible Holes, Bleach Art, Woolly Aphids, Tricks with Gravity, Computer Explosions, and Home Made Fire Extinguishers.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Flour, Pendulum, Leaf Color, How a Siren Works, Blow out Candle, Guitar, Aurora, Crush can and more
programme.desc: n this episode, Mr. Wizard shows Flour Explosion, Pendulum, Hidden Leaf Color, How Sirens Work, Mapping a Guitar, Blown Out Candle, Card Illusion, Aurora Borealis, Come Back Can and Crush Gallon Can.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.sub-title: Speed of Sound, Refraction, Trick, Light Bulb Filament, Glass Duet, Spell Check, Stars and more
programme.desc: Speed of Sound, Selective Refraction Trick, How Many Stars, Glass Duet, Spelling Checker, After a Forest Fire, Window Oscillation, Asparagus, Finding a Tree's Height. Mr. Wizard measures the difference between hearing the sound of a starter pistol over the walkie-talkie and hearing it through the air.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Lester learns the value of a dollar when Beakman tries to make "cents" out of currency systems; and Liza soaks up some information about water power.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Why waste reduction isn't just a bunch of garbage; predicting the weather.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Skyscrapers; the properties of ice; making a chemistry set out of fruits and vegetables.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Searching for the ultimate predator -- sharks; bouncing an egg; Einstein and Beakman compare hairstyles.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Making fungus fun; the world of slugs; the formation of caves.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Frogs, toads, salamanders, newts, and caecilians (worm-like animals that have backbones) are all amphibians, animals that spend part of their lives in water and part on land. Amphibians are slimy. Amphibians are cold-blooded that means their body temperature changes with the temperature outside. And as amphibians grow up, they go through metamorphosis.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Volcanoes are mountains made from molten rock. The Earth’s crust is divided into big slabs, called plates, which are slowly moving all the time. The plates are floating on the Earth’s mantle, a layer of gooey hot rock that flows like maple syrup. Some places in the mantle, the rock gets very hot and nearly liquid. It’s called magma. Sometimes the magma reaches the Earth’s surface and forms a volcano.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball is asked to settle a dispute between Penny and her family.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin find themselves in serious trouble when they agree to everything the new kid in school says. The truth is they can't understand a word that he says. Peter Pepperoni has been home schooled and developed a very odd way of speaking. Due to a series of misunderstandings Gumball and Darwin end up on the run from the law.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: To make ends meet, the Wattersons are forced to rent out their house. However, with nowhere else to stay they decide to secretly remain at the house. When they accidentally surprise their guests, an elderly pair of fainting goats, they faint...unsurprisingly. The Wattersons fear the worst and attempt to cover up this "accident".

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Halloween isn't what it used to be. Can Gumball and Darwin help the ghouls get their scare back?

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Mr. Small goes feral after Gumball and Darwin expose his hypocrisy.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball decides to engross himself in plant culture to appease Leslie after making an offensive comment.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Richard misses a package and is forced into an escalating feud with the delivery guy.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball tries to protect Darwin from his unsuitable undead girlfriend Carrie.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Anais and Jamie form an unlikely partnership when they're accused of a crime they didn't commit.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole has decided it's time to throw out the family's refrigerator. This doesn't go down too well with Richard, as it's his favorite possession. When it turns out the refrigerator is also a magical storage facility for childhood emotions and memories, it makes sense that Richard is having a hard time letting go.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Can the Titans master the four New Year's traditions in order to break the cycle of bad years?

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin is determined to become a cat in order to get Starfire's attention.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After a humiliating defeat, Raven and the Titans work on building their lower body strength, becoming the "League of Legs."

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After Beast Boy gets money from the Tooth Fairy, the other Titans try to get in on the action by knocking out their own teeth.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After learning looks don't matter, Beast Boy undergoes a metamorphosis through a cocoon, becoming an ugly, gross creature.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Having grown sick of Robin's bland cooking, the other Titans make attempts to spice things up.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Stuck inside on a rainy day, the Titans do rainy-day activities to keep from boredom.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Hey You, Don't Forget About Me In Your Memory
programme.desc: When Robin decides to become an all-American class president-type person, he convinces the Titans to return to school with him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Accept The Next Proposition You Hear
programme.desc: Robin refuses to give the Titans any more orders so that they'll learn to think for themselves.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Control Freak informs the Titans that they are on a TV show he created and demands they earn him an award.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg's real strength comes not from his robot parts, but from his favorite song.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy starts his journey into teenagedom, complete with all of its trials & tribulations.
